verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 subunit B) is a core structural component of the ATP synthase complex located on the inner mitochondrial membrane. The protein is synthesized on cytoplasmic ribosomes, imported into mitochondria via the TOM/TIM import machinery, and assembles as part of the F1 catalytic sector of complex V. No documented stable TM anchoring to the plasma membrane, no GPI anchor, no stable non-covalent partnering with surface receptors, and no cancer/cell-death/activation-induced surface translocation pathway reported. Not targeted by any cell-surface-directed therapeutic. No documented dual localization with a PM pool, no lysosomal exocytosis pathway, no tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display. Inner-mitochondrial-membrane residence is its sole canonical localization.
